steps.title: Multiply the angle measure
steps.content:
Angle $K$ is $4$ times angle $J$, so its radian measure is
$$4\cdot \frac{7\pi}{11}=\frac{28\pi}{11}$$

steps.title: Convert radians to degrees
steps.content:
To convert radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180}{\pi}$:
$$\frac{28\pi}{11}\cdot \frac{180}{\pi}=\frac{28\cdot 180}{11}$$

steps.title: Select the matching expression
steps.content: The expression that represents the measure of angle $K$ in degrees is $\frac{7}{11}(180)(4)$. The answer is B.
